parsed_review.summary: CP00-284 is a descriptor-only email-DMS UI binding-tail pack (RP08.P04.M05.S17-RP08.P04.M06.S04, risk A, 10 units) whose production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted (manifest production_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review, flag email_dms_core_ui_binding_tail_descriptor_verified). Implementation, validators, contract projection, tests, and command-evidence are mutually consistent: the CP284 pack binding and requirements (registry.js:217,1205) match the baseline exactly (10 units, deliverable distribution {test:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:3,ui:4}, micro-title split {Permission And Audit Binding:6,Synthetic Fixture Set:4}); all 7 required public exports exist (createEmailDmsCoreCp284UiBindingTailCaseSet/Descriptor in service.js plus 5 validators in validators.js:2737-2906) and are re-surfaced via export * in index.js. freezeCp284Result (service.js:1699) stamps every no-write/no-runtime/no-leak attestation false, and the inherited row-extras chain supplies every field the descriptor validator asserts (state_snapshot_descriptor_only, no_unauthorized_count_leak, permission_decision_detail_included:false). The contract ui_binding_tail_descriptor is deep-equaled by the validate script and carries the correct authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-285) and the full CP178+ invalid_review_blockers list (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, claude_final_approval_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim). Three CP284 tests (model.test.js:801-848) validate coverage, descriptor-only rows, and evidence packets/handoff against the live closeout plan; command-evidence shows the full validation suite, npm test, npm run build, and git diff --check all exit 0, and no conflict markers exist in the package. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob) and is not final approver; Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review-packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent because this review precedes them and review status is pending. Auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte stdout, ok:true) so the review evidence is valid under hardening rules. One non-blocking P3: the changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own evidence dir docs/closeout-packs/cp00-284/ and new-session-handoff.md in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, a cosmetic mislabel that omits no active file and does not weaken any gate. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack is sound for closeout pending the human final approval the pack itself defers to.

findings.title: Pack's own evidence directory double-listed as unrelated in changed-file-scope.json
findings.details: In changed-file-scope.json, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-284/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). The cp00-284/ directory is this pack's own evidence (manifest.json + command-evidence.json), so labeling it 'unrelated' is contradictory. This appears to stem from an overloaded field name ('preserved' = left in worktree, not reverted) rather than a real scoping error: active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, no required active implementation file is dropped, and the manifest's own worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:524-527) correctly lists only the .docx and the ldip c-ldip-01 file. It does not affect the production_ready claim, any validation gate, or the no-write/no-real-data boundary.
findings.recommendation: Cosmetic only: have the scope generator exclude the active pack's own evidence directory (docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/) and its handoff doc from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the two categories are mutually exclusive, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy. No action required for CP00-284 closeout.
